_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Conduct exploitation of wireless computer and digital networks.
reference_document_element_description: Conduct threat model assessments for AI systems, identifying AI-specific attack surfaces, threat actors, and applicable mitigating controls.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI RM domain. RM-T-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.312), SP-K-001 (SP, cosine: 0.293).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.6/10 (raw 0.264). Shared vocabulary: [conduct]. DCWF-only terms [computer, digital, exploitation, networks, wireless] vs WIDAI-only terms [actors, ai, applicable, assessments, attack, controls] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.880 (P=0.898, R=0.862). P exceeds R by 0.037 — the DCWF element's 8 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 17 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (72.0%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Conduct exploitation of wireless computer and digital networks." and "Conduct threat model assessments for AI systems, identifying AI-specific attack". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.316 > STS 0.264 (gap +0.052). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [conduct] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 9.1%. Shared tokens: [conduct]. DCWF-only: [computer, digital, exploitation, networks, wireless]. WIDAI-only: [actors, ai, applicable, assessments, attack, controls].
